acceptance_targets: [CH1-BOOKER-CONTINUITY-HOLLIS-001] PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:249 no longer names or implies Hollis as physically present in chapter 1 (or canon is explicitly updated to include chapter 1); FAIL if 'Hollis' remains scene-active in chapter 1 without canon change.

[CH1-CRAFT-SANITIZATION-INTERVIEW-MONTAGE-002] PASS if, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:193, at least one witness interview is rendered as a full scene (minimum three back-and-forth lines plus embodied fear detail and a leverage shift); FAIL if the section remains montage-level summary.

[CH1-CRAFT-TIMIDITY-AFTERMATH-CUTAWAY-003] PASS if the section between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:163 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 includes an explicit immediate consequence tied to the threat (observable worker reaction plus concrete protective or failed protective action); FAIL if it still jumps directly from threat to generic canvass setup.

[FBR-02-005] Pass if chapter_01 keeps a single 'went still' usage at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:279 and the immediate follow-up remains scene-specific; fail if additional stillness-template lines are added in chapter_01.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:249 no longer names or implies Hollis as physically present in chapter 1 (or canon is explicitly updated to include chapter 1); FAIL if 'Hollis' remains scene-active in chapter 1 without canon change.
findings.evidence: Continuity sheet limits Hollis on-page availability to chapters 9, 14, 17-18, but chapter text places him at the scene in chapter 1: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:249.
findings.problem: Canonical continuity contradiction: Deputy Hollis is functionally present in chapter 1 despite being unavailable until chapter 9 in the continuity pack. This destabilizes downstream reveal timing and weakens planned escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, remove direct Hollis attribution and replace with a non-canonical deputy placeholder (e.g., unnamed deputy or Deputy Kane already established at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:205 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:253), or update the continuity sheet canon if Hollis must be present from chapter 1.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:193, at least one witness interview is rendered as a full scene (minimum three back-and-forth lines plus embodied fear detail and a leverage shift); FAIL if the section remains montage-level summary.
findings.evidence: The intimidation-heavy witness phase is compressed into summary: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:171; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:191.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: the text reports collective fear/probable coercion instead of staging one full early interview where pressure, evasion, and bodily consequence are rendered in-scene. Procedural summary replaces dramatic presence at a moment the chapter premise requires specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the compressed block around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:171, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:191 by inserting one complete pre-Calvin interview that dramatizes fear mechanics (who is watching, what the witness fears losing, how speech changes under threat) before the Calvin turn at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:193.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the section between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:163 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169 includes an explicit immediate consequence tied to the threat (observable worker reaction plus concrete protective or failed protective action); FAIL if it still jumps directly from threat to generic canvass setup.
findings.evidence: After explicit lynching threat, the narrative quickly shifts to process cadence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:163; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:167; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169.
findings.problem: Timidity: the prose establishes immediate racist violence risk, then retreats into procedural transition without dwelling in immediate emotional/operational consequence for threatened workers in that beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, add a short consequence beat before the noon transition showing immediate protective action and witness condition (e.g., Terrence's state, deputy response failure, Lena's tactical adjustment) so the threat lands as lived reality, not bridge text.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter_01 keeps a single 'went still' usage at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:279 and the immediate follow-up remains scene-specific; fail if additional stillness-template lines are added in chapter_01.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book construction: the default stillness line ('room/face went still') appears in 4 chapters (chapter_01, chapter_03, chapter_06, chapter_16), becoming a prose tic. KEEP this chapter's instance as the anchor; replace the others.
findings.rewrite_direction: Retain the anchor beat at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:279, but sharpen adjacent reaction detail at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:281 so this instance carries unique pressure texture and does not read as reusable template.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the version that preserves uncomfortable specificity and character accountability; do not replace difficult scenes with procedural summary, and do not mute class, race, sexuality, or institutional cruelty when those forces are actively shaping choices.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene with violence, racism, grief, or moral compromise is rendered in abstract or polite shorthand, revise to restore specific bodily, social, and material consequence before accepting the draft.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in contemporary Mississippi speech; uncontracted forms are reserved for formality, emphasis, or social performance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: rotate structural textures across chapters: procedural surge, quiet domestic interval, document-driven slow burn, dread preparation, aftermath inventory, and information-turn chapters; avoid repeating scene count plus dialogue density pattern in adjacent chapters.
prose_style_profile.diction: literary but grounded; precise legal and forensic terms when needed, balanced by lived vernacular and local material detail.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored primarily to Lena, with brief controlled expansions only when factual clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: alternate compressed procedural sentences with longer sensory-emotional lines so pressure and reflection keep rebalancing.
